raw_customer_language: The tub spout keeps dripping even when the fixture is off.
It has a hot knob, a middle knob, and a cold knob.
The middle knob sends the water up to the shower or back down to the tub.
I do not know the brand, but there is an access panel behind the shower.
Do you charge a diagnostic fee, and what does something like this usually cost?
reported_symptoms: tub spout drips while fixture is off
reported_fixture_details: middle knob controls tub or shower flow

inferred_fields.known_equipment_summary: Three-handle tub/shower fixture with separate hot, diverter, and cold controls.
inferred_fields.confidence_note: Planning-only confidence labels are review aids and not canonical runtime values.

ambiguities.reason: The exact internal failure mode is not confirmed from the synthetic interaction alone.
Brand remains unknown until a human or site inspection confirms it.
The access panel is reported, but technician usability is not yet verified.

missing_information: whether dripping is continuous or intermittent
whether any recent repair attempt has been made

policy_questions.customer_language: Do you charge a diagnostic fee?
What does something like this usually cost?

review_recommendations: Confirm that the inferred service category is appropriate before operational use.
Keep brand as unknown unless a human verifies it.
Verify that the access panel is usable and note any access constraints.
Keep diagnostic fee and pricing questions separate from job symptoms.
Treat the 1:00 PM request as a preference and not a scheduling commitment.
